prompt:
Develop a LocalOps Merchant Booking & HR Operations system for offline-first teams that need to manage merchant content, bookings, and hiring workflows from a single browser-based workspace. The application runs as a responsive Single Page Application built with Svelte, using client-side routing to move between Merchant Console, Booking Desk, Recruiting, and Org Admin areas without reloads. Users authenticate via a local username and password screen (no email/SMS), with roles including Administrator, Merchant Editor, Content Reviewer/Publisher, Booking Agent, HR Manager, and Recruiter. Merchant Editors maintain structured merchant, store, menu, and combo records using Table views with inline editing, Drawer-based detail panels, and Modal review prompts; images are added via file picker with size limits (max 5 MB per image, JPEG/PNG only) and tag/amenity selection. A draft-review-publish workflow requires a Reviewer to approve changes before they become “Published,” and users can compare versions side-by-side. Booking Agents manage availability calendars and place reservations using a guided booking flow that shows conflicts in real time, supports reschedules, and enforces cancellation rules such as “free cancellation up to 2 hours before start; otherwise mark as Late Cancel.” Recruiters create offers from templates, route them for approval, capture e-signatures (typed name plus optional drawn signature on a Canvas), collect onboarding documents with field validation (e.g., SSN must match ###-##-#### format and is masked on screen), and track onboarding checklists with statuses Not Started/In Progress/Complete. Team collaboration is handled through an in-app conversation panel with context history, canned responses, shared notes, archiving, and local search by keyword and date range.

All data persistence is local-only, using IndexedDB as the primary “database,” with LocalStorage reserved for lightweight preferences (theme, last workspace) and a file-based import/export mechanism for backups and bulk operations. Bulk import/export supports CSV/JSON files processed entirely in-browser, and exports are generated as downloadable Blobs (e.g., “merchants_03-25-2026.json”) to enable secure transfer via USB when needed. Consistency and oversell prevention are implemented with IndexedDB transactions plus an application lock manager that coordinates across multiple tabs using BroadcastChannel; order/booking creation must be idempotent by client-generated idempotency keys stored for 24 hours to block duplicate submissions. Seat/slot holds are time-limited (default TTL 10 minutes for orders, 5 minutes for booking locks) and automatically rolled back on timeout or browser crash via recovery checks at app startup. Organizational hierarchy is stored as department-grade-class-organization trees with position dictionaries (responsibilities, eligibility rules, headcount limits) and occupancy statistics computed on demand, with all critical actions recorded in an immutable local audit trail including timestamp, actor, previous state, and new state. Sensitive fields (password hashes, SSN, offer compensation) are encrypted at rest using a locally derived key, and the UI enforces auto-lock after 15 minutes of inactivity with a re-auth Modal.

Frontend: Svelte (SPA) using client-side routing, Svelte Stores for state, and HTML5 Canvas for e-signatures.

Backend: In-browser Logic (Service Workers for background tasks, Web Lock API for concurrency, and BroadcastChannel for cross-tab sync).

Database: IndexedDB (via Dexie.js for ACID transactions) as primary storage; LocalStorage for UI preferences.
backend_framework: In-browser service worker + Web Lock API + BroadcastChannel
